item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I actually really like that, it is more realistic. But I would include a line or two of dialogue from the mother to incorporate the realism into the interview in a clearer way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thank you, I've enlisted help for a proofread in the grammar and spelling. I'll be re-writing very shortly.

In the interview with Cynthia and Andy, having her listed was intentional. Since Andy is a minor, I felt it would be unrealistic to have him questioned without his mother's presence.

(Edit)

Re-write has been completed. Fixed grammatical and spelling errors. Also added content to Donna and Andy's interviews. Simon's final excerpt has also been re-written.

item.title: Junior Staff - Site Crit
item.content:encoded:
Here Be Crit / Opinion
ByeBye

Hey 4esthetics, I have a few pointers I'd like to share.

Okay, so this spot is where I would normally point out the missed spelling and grammar mistakes but this has far too many for me to list out. I highly recommend a program called Grammarly it helps greatly for minor SPaG mistakes and I believe a free version is available on most platforms.

So, in my opinion, the dialogue here falls a bit flat, I would suggest going over it with someone experienced in writing authentic dialogue since the interviews are really the bread and butter of this article. While on the subject of the interviews In the second interview, the one with andy, it says both Andrew and Cynthia are being interviewed and only Andy says anything.

I feel you had a really neat concept here, but more could have been done during the writing process to really make it shine. I highly suggest taking advantage of the writing forums and #thecritters on IRC chat; proper critique and thorough reviewing is a good way to really bring out the best article you can. After all, we have a community of great writers here and its a shame not to use that to your advantage. For the reasons listed above its a no-vote from me though I hope to see more articles from you in the future



Disclaimer: Opinions expressed in this review do not reflect the opinions of SCP Wiki staff as a whole. Please respond to DrAkimoto or contact the captain of the Site Criticism Team, SoullessSingularity, for any questions or concerns relating to this review.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like it, but it could be better.

There's decent narrative flow. the dialogue feels a bit wonky at times but I like the ending. the clinical tone is also iffy sometimes, but it's good enough for a hesitant +1 from me. I like the idea, but the errors make the quality worse. Good job!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My feelings on this article can best be defined as "eh". It's not bad, but it's not good either. The final part also feels a bit forced and the article takes a while to get to the point. No vote for now.
